signal.headline: Storm-1516 deploys Euronews impersonation against Hungary as April 12 election enters critical phase — AI-generated YouTube network simultaneously farming 1.8 billion views with fabricated war narratives
signal.note: MF1 alert: pro-Orbán Hungarian Conservative outlet running counter-narrative attempting to delegitimise the interference story itself — a meta-FIMI layer on the primary operation

campaigns.summary: SDA confirmed operating from Russian Embassy Budapest. Operations include fake local news seeding, pro-Orbán narrative amplification. Magyar explicitly named TikTok as primary vector. 11 days to election. EEAS RAS active.
campaigns.changelog: [2026-04-02: Storm-1516 Euronews impersonation confirmed as parallel operation targeting same election — RU-004 added as linked campaign]

campaigns.summary: Meta February 2026 CIB removed 1,700+ assets. No new confirmed activity since takedown. Infrastructure reconstitution likely but not yet evidenced at Tier 1/2. Status remains DISRUPTED.

campaigns.operation_name: GRU — UK Political Party Funding Investigation
campaigns.summary: MI5-referenced investigation ongoing. No new primary-source intelligence this week. Status unchanged.

campaigns.platform: X, Facebook, Telegram, fake news websites
campaigns.target: Hungary — April 12 election; opposition leader Péter Magyar
campaigns.summary: Researchers at the Gnida project confirmed Storm-1516 is running a coordinated impersonation campaign using fake Euronews-style websites and videos. Fabricated article claimed Magyar insulted Trump and called him 'senile grandpa'. Video spread across coordinated anonymous accounts. Storm-1516 previously active in 2024 US presidential election and Germany's February 2025 elections. MF1 alert: Hungarian Conservative counter-narrative attempting to delegitimise the interference story itself.
campaigns.changelog: [2026-04-02: New campaign — Storm-1516 Euronews impersonation confirmed by Gnida project researchers]

campaigns.target: Hungary — election narrative; Orbán legitimacy amplification
campaigns.summary: Washington Post (21 March) reported SVR proposed staging a fake assassination attempt against Orbán to shift election into 'emotional realm' of security and stability. Plan internally named 'Gamechanger'. WaPo cites single unnamed European intelligence document — no independent corroboration. MF3 alert: single-source, unverified document. Attribution: Assessed (consistent with SVR active measures doctrine but not independently confirmed). Counter-narrative from pro-Orbán sources attempts to delegitimise story entirely — assess as motivated counter-narrative, not exculpatory evidence.
campaigns.changelog: [2026-04-02: New entry — MF3 caution applied: single unnamed intelligence source, no independent corroboration]

campaigns.summary: Google TAG Q1 2026 disruption remains the last confirmed enforcement action. No new primary-source intelligence this week. Reconstitution ongoing but not yet confirmed at Tier 1/2.

campaigns.operation_name: United Front — Pacific Island States Outreach
campaigns.summary: No new primary-source intelligence this week. Status unchanged.

campaigns.operation_name: AI-Generated YouTube Network — War Coverage Slop
campaigns.target: English and Russian-speaking audiences; Russia-Ukraine war narrative
campaigns.summary: DFRLab (23 March 2026): 26 YouTube channels, collectively 1.8 billion views, 1.96 million subscribers. Deploys AI synthetic anchors, LLM-generated scripts, automated thumbnails. Blends factual geopolitical reporting with fabricated events (invented strikes on Mykolaiv, Rzeszów). Network coordinated: synchronized pauses/reactivations, content mirroring, thematic pivots from Armenia-Azerbaijan to Ukraine. 23 of 26 channels still active as of 23 March. Attribution: Assessed as coordinated network (behaviour confirmed); state attribution not yet established at Tier 1/2 — UNATTRIBUTED pending further evidence. MF2 alert: do not assume single-actor attribution solely from content alignment.
campaigns.changelog: [2026-04-02: New campaign — DFRLab March 23 report. Actor field: UNATTRIBUTED pending Tier 1/2 attribution. MF2 caution applied.]

campaigns.target: EU and US university campus environments
campaigns.summary: No new primary-source intelligence this week. Status unchanged.
No new primary-source intelligence this week. Status unchanged.

campaigns.summary: No new primary-source intelligence this week. AoS infrastructure remains active. Status unchanged.

No new primary-source intelligence this week. NSO Group permanent injunction (October 2025) and appeal (November 2025) noted under commercial operators — separate from Black Cube but relevant to commercial cognitive warfare sector status. Status unchanged.

No new confirmed operations this week. Campus amplification ongoing.

platform_responses.headline: 88% of EU-flagged FIMI content remains on X (EEAS Mar 2026)
AI-generated network (26 channels, 1.8B views) remains active despite misinformation policy violations — no confirmed enforcement action

attribution_log.note: SVR Gamechanger: single unnamed European intelligence document, no independent corroboration. Do not upgrade to Confirmed without second Tier 1/2 source.

AI YouTube network: coordination confirmed (DFRLab behavioural analysis), state attribution not yet established. Content alignment with Russian narratives is necessary but not sufficient for attribution. Retain UNATTRIBUTED until Tier 1/2 source attributes.

Storm-1516 Euronews impersonation: MF1 alert — Hungarian Conservative counter-narrative attempting to delegitimise this story. Counter-narrative is motivated (pro-Orbán editorial stance) and does not constitute exculpatory evidence. Primary attribution via Gnida project researchers maintained.

cognitive_warfare.theme: AI-generated synthetic media as scalable FIMI infrastructure
cognitive_warfare.development: DFRLab March 2026 documentation of 26-channel YouTube network (1.8B views) using AI synthetic anchors, LLM scripts, automated thumbnails represents operational proof-of-concept for AI-at-scale FIMI. Production cost per video negligible; reach algorithmic. This is the maturation of the AI-FIMI convergence flagged in Issue 1 (47% of EEAS-tracked operations AI-assisted). The YouTube model differs from text-seeding operations: video with synthetic anchors creates higher epistemic authority signals for audiences.
cognitive_warfare.significance: Structural — represents the AI capability transition from augmentation to primary production pipeline

cognitive_warfare.development: Hungarian Conservative's March 23 article systematically contests the evidence base for Russian interference reporting ahead of the April 12 election. Consistent with known Russian active measures doctrine: seed doubt about the interference story itself, not just the primary narratives. This meta-layer — discrediting the attributors rather than the attributed operation — is MF1 territory and analytically distinct from legitimate methodological critique.
cognitive_warfare.significance: Tactical — election-specific, but the meta-FIMI pattern is structurally persistent across all documented Russian electoral operations

cognitive_warfare.theme: EEAS Hybrid Rapid Response Team — Armenia June 2026
cognitive_warfare.development: EEAS HRVP Kallas confirmed at the 2026 Counter-FIMI Conference (17 March) that a Hybrid Rapid Response Team will deploy to Armenia ahead of June 2026 parliamentary elections. First HRRТ deployment to the South Caucasus. Armenia was identified in EEAS FIMI Report 4th as an expected primary target in the run-up to June elections. Signals a doctrinal shift from post-election attribution to pre-election deterrence.
cognitive_warfare.significance: Doctrinal — EEAS shifting from reactive attribution to proactive pre-election deployment

flags.headline: FIMI as constitutive mechanism of democratic erosion — Hungary election FIMI density at critical level
flags.linkage: Three simultaneous confirmed/assessed RU operations targeting Hungary April 12 election (SDA, Storm-1516, SVR Gamechanger) represent the highest documented pre-election FIMI density for a single EU election. The Democratic Integrity Monitor's Hungary election-adjacent entries should cross-reference this cluster. F1-flagged signal this week.

flags.headline: Cognitive warfare as operational preparation for European strategic paralysis — Hungary election as test case
flags.linkage: If Orbán retains power with confirmed Russian FIMI support, this consolidates a Russian-aligned veto actor inside the EU for the next cycle. The EGHTM tracks EU institutional coherence — this is the FIMI dimension of that question. Washington Post reporting on Szijjártó leaking EU meeting content to Lavrov (if confirmed) represents the intelligence capture dimension that EGHTM state-capture tracker should note.

flags.headline: AI-generated YouTube network (1.8B views) as operational proof of AI-FIMI convergence at scale
flags.linkage: DFRLab March 23 documentation of 26-channel AI production pipeline achieving 1.8B views is the most significant empirical data point on AI-FIMI convergence since the EEAS 47% figure. AI Governance Monitor should track platform policy gaps (YouTube not enforcing against synthetic anchors presenting fabricated events as news) as a governance failure with direct FIMI consequences.
